method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA; nyccfb.info returns no robots.txt — 404). Fingerprinted nyccfb.info (Microsoft IIS 8.5 + ASP.NET MVC 5.2, X-AspNetMvc-Version 5.2 / X-AspNet-Version 4.0.30319), the Follow the Money search app (AngularJS 1.5 SPA over an ASP.NET Web API at nyccfb.info/FTMSearchWebAPI, confirmed returning live JSON from /api/Common/GetElectionCycle), the bulk CSV Data Library (/DataLibrary/*.csv, 56 files, 2001-2025), nycvotes.org (Umbraco CMS on ASP.NET / Azure App Service — UMB_SESSION cookie, ARRAffinity, Application Insights), and the login-walled C-SMART, IEDS, and C-Access ASP.NET applications. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Campaign Finance Board (CFB)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 16 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: CFB is the transparency leader among NYC agencies and is *almost* API-first already: it runs on its own domain (nyccfb.info, not nyc.gov), publishes 16 NYC Open Data datasets, ships a bulk Data Library of per-cycle CSVs back to 2001, and its Follow the Money search is a single-page app backed by a REAL, working JSON Web API (nyccfb.info/FTMSearchWebAPI).
But that API is undocumented, backend-only, and unowned as a product — no OpenAPI, no developer docs, no stable public contract, no agent-native surface. It is a de-facto API, not a published one.

The reference/analysis data is exceptionally rich: contributions with full intermediary/bundler detail (rjkp-yttg, 54 columns), expenditures (qxzj-vkn2), public matching-funds payments (u69g-mvrb), and per-candidate financial analysis (m3tj-a2pb) — in parallel citywide and off-year/special-election streams.

The write layer is locked. Filing a disclosure statement happens only inside C-SMART, the CFB's login-walled web-based disclosure application; there is no submission API and no Open Data twin (only Late/Missing Disclosure Statements, 2ujk-6z7u, exposes filing status).
Net-new write surface: submit a disclosure statement (DisclosureFiling) — the one campaign-finance transaction with no machine-readable contract today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.type: backend JSON Web API (ASP.NET Web API)
apis_observed.note: Powers the Follow the Money AngularJS search. Confirmed live JSON from /api/Common/GetElectionCycle, plus /api/Common/* reference lists and /api/AutoComplete/* typeaheads for candidates, contributors, payees, intermediaries, and independent spenders. Real API, no OpenAPI/docs — the core finding.
16 CFB datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. Contributions, expenditures, intermediaries, public funds, financial analysis, enforcement, voter analysis.
56 flat CSV files (contribution, expenditure, payment, intermediary, analysis) per election cycle from 2001 through 2025. Bulk twin of the Open Data / FTM data.

Web-based application campaigns use to disclose (submit) contributions/expenditures — i.e. file. The net-new write surface; no JSON/OpenAPI. Was showing an 'unavailable' maintenance notice at crawl time.
Independent Expenditure Disclosure System — a separate login-walled filing app for outside spenders.
Access portal referenced from the FTM search header.
The public voter-engagement brand; Umbraco CMS / ASP.NET on Azure App Service. contribute.nycvotes.org and nycvotes.turbovote.org are separate services. No public content API observed.
appInsights telemetry injected on the FTM search page.

meta.platform: Microsoft IIS 8.5 / ASP.NET MVC 5.2 + AngularJS 1.5 (nyccfb.info + FTMSearchWebAPI); Umbraco CMS / ASP.NET on Azure (nycvotes.org); C-SMART / IEDS / C-Access ASP.NET applications
meta.netnew: DisclosureFiling — a filer submits a disclosure statement (today only via the login-walled C-SMART disclosure application)
meta.tagline: CFB already publishes its campaign-finance data every way except as a documented, owned, agent-native API.
meta.headline: The city's transparency leader runs a real but undocumented search API and locks the one write it owns — filing a disclosure — inside C-SMART.

scorecard_notes.platform: Own domain, coherent stack, but legacy IIS 8.5 / ASP.NET MVC 5.2 / AngularJS 1.5 and ASP.NET WebForms filing apps.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: A real, working JSON Web API (FTMSearchWebAPI) exists — but undocumented, backend-only, no OpenAPI or contract.
scorecard_notes.openData: 16 Socrata datasets + bulk per-cycle CSV Data Library (2001-2025) + public dashboards; among the most open in NYC.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Contributions (with bundler detail), expenditures, public funds, and per-candidate financial analysis all richly published.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: Filing a disclosure statement is only possible inside the login-walled C-SMART app; no submission API, no Open Data twin.
scorecard_notes.agent: No MCP, no agent-native surface, no documented API for an agent to call.
scorecard_notes.ownership: CFB owns its domain, its search app, its data library, and its API — very high self-ownership, unlike rented or hidden stacks.

fruit.name: Campaign Contributions
fruit.machine_readable: Open Data (SODA) + FTM Web API + bulk CSV
opendata_match: Off-Year and Special Elections Contribution kgzj-pjna (52c)
fruit.notes: The most-searched CFB record. Full donor detail plus intermediary/bundler fields (INTERMNAME, INT* columns) and matchable amounts (MATCHAMNT). Served three ways: Socrata, the FTM Web API, and per-cycle CSV.

fruit.name: Campaign Expenditures
fruit.machine_readable: Open Data (SODA) + FTM Web API + bulk CSV
opendata_match: Off-Year and Special Elections Campaign Expenditure cgcg-w2ys (32c)
fruit.notes: Campaign spending by payee, purpose code, and date. Payee typeahead is exposed by the FTM Web API (/api/AutoComplete/GetPayees).

fruit.name: Campaign Intermediaries (bundlers)
opendata_match: Off-Year and Special Elections Intermediary yqhh-93zq (24c)
fruit.notes: Bundlers who deliver contributions; folded into the Contribution schema's intermediary object. Intermediary typeahead in the FTM Web API.

fruit.name: Campaign Public Funds Payments
opendata_match: Off-Year and Special Elections Public Matching Fund Payments 7f4s-uwi7 (12c)
fruit.notes: Matching-funds payments (early/primary/runoff/general/total) under NYC's up-to-8-to-1 small-dollar program — the flagship CFB output.

fruit.name: Campaign Financial Analysis (candidates)
opendata_match: Off-Year and Special Elections Financial Analysis ups9-zwkm (26c)
fruit.notes: Per-candidate rollup: contributions, matchable amounts, public funds, expenditures, limits, outstanding bills. The Candidate entity's financial summary. Candidate typeahead in the FTM Web API.

fruit.name: Follow the Money search API
fruit.notes: The real-but-undocumented ASP.NET Web API behind the Follow the Money AngularJS app. Confirmed live JSON. Reference lists (offices, cycles, statement types) and autocomplete for candidates/contributors/payees/intermediaries/independent spenders. Needs documenting + owning as a product, not building from scratch.

fruit.name: Late/Missing Disclosure Statements
fruit.notes: The only machine-readable window onto disclosure FILING status (Statement, Due Date, Days Late, Status). Read-only and partial; the filing content and submission itself have no API.

fruit.name: Enforcement & audit determinations
opendata_match: Enforcement Actions Board Determinations and Penalties xrxs-qn95 (6c)
fruit.notes: Post-election penalties and audit findings — the compliance backend to disclosure filing.

fruit.name: Voter analysis & turnout (NYC Votes)
fruit.notes: The voter-engagement side of the CFB (NYC Votes brand). Rich but tangential to the campaign-finance core; the nycvotes.org site itself exposes no public content API.

fruit.name: File a disclosure statement (C-SMART)
fruit.notes: The net-new WRITE surface — a filer submits a periodic disclosure statement. Today only through the login-walled C-SMART web application (IEDS for independent spenders); no submission API, no OpenAPI, no JSON. The one campaign-finance transaction with no machine-readable contract.
